summary: A compact defensive tower structure with two tall wooden pillars flanking a central dark wood core. The building features tiered overhanging roofs at multiple levels, a stone brick foundation platform, and small window openings. The design suggests a watchtower or fortified gatehouse with asymmetrical side wings.

shape.overall_form: Two vertical towers with central connecting structure and asymmetrical side wings
shape.symmetry: Asymmetrical; right tower taller than left, with offset wing extensions

rebuild_hints: Build two main vertical wooden towers of unequal height (right taller than left)
Create a central dark wood core connecting the towers
Construct stone brick foundation platform extending around base perimeter
Add tiered wooden roof overhangs at multiple levels, staggered outward
Place small glass windows on tower faces at regular intervals
Add asymmetrical wing extensions on left and right sides
Use wooden slabs for roof overhang details and layering
Ensure overhangs extend progressively outward at each level
uncertainties: Exact interior layout and room divisions not visible from exterior views
Precise block-by-block dimensions difficult to determine; estimate based on visual proportions
Specific wood type variants (oak vs dark oak) uncertain from texture alone
Roof material composition (slabs vs stairs vs full blocks) partially obscured
Foundation depth and internal support structure not visible
Exact window placement pattern on rear/interior faces unknown
